# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |
|-------|-------|--------|------------------|-----------|
| #647 | codex-preflight: parseTomlTableName cannot parse quoted table headers — table-state leak silently misreports multi_agent_v2 as disabled and leaks numeric bounds (×7 files) | open | — | parseTomlTableName's header regex ([A-Za-z0-9_.-]+ only) returns null on standard-TOML quoted headers ([projects."/path"], [plugins."name@mkt"]) which Codex itself writes, so the line-scanner's table state sticks on the last unquoted table; a later enabled= key under a plugins block then trips the duplicate-key ambiguity guard and detectCodexDispatchMode reports a v2-enabled machine as v1-thread-id (reproduced live 2026-07-09, control passes without quoted tables), degrading the whole run's dispatch choreography (mode flows claim → workflow-state → every dispatch card → plan-run v2 branch); second arm: parseMultiAgentV2NumericFields adopts numeric keys from unrelated later quoted tables (repro'd 99 from an mcp_servers block). Compounding: MULTI_AGENT_V2_BOUNDS_NOTE recommends the dotted-table form that triggers Arm 1 (and is scalar/table-invalid TOML from the common multi_agent_v2=true start); only the undocumented inline-object form works. Fix: extend parseTomlTableName to accept quoted-segment + [[array]] headers (state must always reset; name fidelity not required — callers only compare features/features.multi_agent_v2); keep the ambiguity guard; switch the note's example to the inline form; regression fixtures for both arms. ×7 lock-step files (4× preflight + 3× installer) → four-chain. Filed by agent (user-approved after adversarial review) 2026-07-09; full analysis on GitHub #647. |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
